name: Colonial Field Breeches
description: A pair of undyed linen knee breeches tied at the calf, practical for daily labor, travel, and life in an early Atlantic settlement.

name: Sailor’s Canvas Slops
description: Loose blue canvas slops with a buttoned fall front, made for sailors working the decks of merchant and fishing vessels.

name: Frontier Work Trousers
description: Durable brown trousers built for rough paths, field work, and travel between coastal settlements and inland routes.

name: Leather Riding Breeches
description: Supple leather knee breeches designed for riders, messengers, and surveyors who spent long days on horseback.

name: Striped Dock Slops
description: Blue-and-cream striped work slops associated with port labor and maritime trade, cut wide for practical movement.

name: Homespun Wool Trousers
description: Plain brown homespun trousers, mended and worn like those of a farmer, apprentice, or smallholder.

name: White Linen Breeches
description: Fresh white linen knee breeches with a formal cut, appropriate for a church service, civic meeting, or town celebration.

name: Indigo Trader Trousers
description: Deep indigo trousers with a sturdy buttoned front, fitting for a trader, ship’s clerk, or prosperous dockside merchant.

name: Traveler’s Road Breeches
description: Hard-wearing road breeches in weathered brown cloth, made for long walks, wagon routes, and changing weather.

name: Merchant’s Dress Trousers
description: Well-kept cream trousers with a tailored waist, suited to a merchant presenting goods, accounts, or correspondence in town.
